content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a decorator that converts the output of a function to uppercase. The decorator should be named 'to_uppercase' and it should be applied to a function called 'greet'.

The 'greet' function takes a name as input and returns a greeting message. Your task is to implement the 'to_uppercase' decorator so that it converts the output of the 'greet' function to uppercase before returning it.

For example, if the input is "Alice", the 'greet' function might return "Hello, Alice!", but with the 'to_uppercase' decorator, it should return "HELLO, ALICE!".

Implement the decorator and apply it to the 'greet' function to solve this task.
constraints: Names contain only letters A‑Z or a‑z
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single name consisting of up to 100 English letters (no spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with the greeting "HELLO, <NAME>!" where <NAME> is the given name converted to uppercase.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each name is inserted into the template "Hello, <name>!" and then converted to uppercase.
First name stays "Z", second name becomes four "Z" characters after uppercasing.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, reading a name. Build the string "Hello, <name>!". Convert the whole string to uppercase (e.g., using toupper in C/C++, toUpperCase in Java, .upper() in Python, .toUpperCase() in JavaScript). Print the result. The algorithm is linear in the total length of all names.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of input) – each character is visited a constant number of times.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.

code.Java:
import java.io.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();for(int i=0;i<T;i++){String name=br.readLine().trim();String res="Hello, "+name+"!".toUpperCase();out.append(res);if(i<T-1) out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
